message:
docs(schema): fix three stale doc nits + differentiate status alias note

Found on a second fresh-eyes review pass:

1. **Stale module docstring.** Said only "JSON Schema documents", omitted
   the per-command envelope map that landed in a58633d3. Rewrote to
   describe both surfaces and reiterate the not-yet-stable warning.
2. **Stale `execute` docstring.** Updated to describe what the function
   actually does now (builds both per-row schemas and per-command map,
   short-circuits in Quiet mode).
3. **Reference to a test that doesn't exist.** `build_commands` doc
   pointed at `commands_match_observed_output` — a name from an earlier
   draft that never made it in. The actual integrity check is
   `command_shapes_have_consistent_invariants`. Pointed there instead
   and described what it actually validates.
4. **`status` alias was indistinguishable from `stats`.** Both keys had
   identical `notes: "Single aggregate object."`, so an agent looking
   up `commands.status` couldn't tell it was an alias for `stats`.
   Differentiated: `stats` keeps the descriptive note; `status` now
   says `"Alias for \`stats\` — identical envelope."` so agents can
   pick the canonical name when one is needed.

The `stats_and_status_share_the_same_envelope` test deliberately does
not check `notes`, so the differentiated text doesn't break the
shape-equality contract — it just adds a name-resolution hint.

Verified: 6 schema tests pass, `cargo fmt --check` clean.
